ops.title: cc-transcript: multi-heredoc lines expose body text as command parts
ops.description:
Pre-existing parse-layer bug surfaced by the splice-layer review (repo: ~/Code/cc-transcript): `cat <<A <<B
one
A
two
B
echo done` walks to parts ["cat","two","B","echo"] — heredoc body/delimiter text becomes Command parts, and with CommandLine.splice they are now writable (corrupting the heredoc). Unreachable for the ccx guard pack (guards match specific executables), but a correctness bug. Fixing the walk changes command_to_dict output → Rust parity golden + crate must move in lockstep (the reason it was deferred).

ops.text: multi-heredoc lines parse to only real command parts, with the Rust parity golden regenerated in the same change
